svg.text: CASE STUDY — SUPPLY CHAIN ATTACK & DEX/BRIDGE LAUNDERING AT SCALE
Case AML-IR-2026-006 · Bybit · $1.46B stolen · Lazarus Group (DPRK) · February 21, 2025 · Largest crypto heist in history
to plain ETH via DEX (14:29 UTC)
Only ChainArgos found it weeks later
$263M laundered via PancakeSwap. Zero initial reports
mentioned it. ChainArgos found it weeks later.
Deliberate high-frequency txs to overwhelm compliance
teams. TRM/FBI confirmed — never documented as ACAMS
155% ETH deposit spike on THORChain was detectable
without a single tagged address. Macro monitoring
could have alerted law enforcement 2 days earlier.
Protocol kept $5M in fees from illicit flows. No refund.
No regulatory action. Creates perverse incentive for
Tether froze immediately. Circle waited 5 hours despite
having the technical ability to freeze USDC. ZachXBT:
"Circle is a bad actor." No regulatory consequence.
eXch announced closure May 2025 under pressure — but
no regulatory action, no seizure, no prosecution. Sets
dangerous precedent for non-compliant instant exchanges.
AML Takeaway: Detection is only as strong as the
weakest link in the ecosystem response chain.
AML Red Flags — Supply Chain + DEX/Bridge Laundering

g.text: • Supply chain attack on wallet signing UI — predicate offence confirmed (ACAMS 2.1 · FATF R.3)
• Immediate DEX swap of illiquid tokens (stETH → ETH) within minutes of theft — ACAMS 4.3.3
• Cross-chain bridge anomaly: +155% ETH deposits to THORChain — detectable via volume monitoring
• "Flood the zone": high-frequency txs to overwhelm investigators — new typology 2025
• Fan-out to 4,876+ BTC addresses — creates 4,876 simultaneous investigation trails
• No-KYC instant exchange (eXch) as intermediary — FATF R.15 · ACAMS 5.1.1
• Stablecoin freeze delay (Circle) enabled continued laundering — FATF R.20 obligation
Frameworks: ACAMS 4.3.3 · FATF VA §5.3 · BSA/FinCEN · IEEPA · DORA · GENIUS Act 2025
Volume anomaly monitoring is the key gap — blockchain forensics must evolve beyond address tagging

svg.text: Recommendations — What I Would Implement as AML Analyst

g.text: 1. Macro volume monitoring on DEX/bridges: flag any protocol with +50% volume spike vs. 30-day average — no address tagging required
2. Stablecoin issuer SLA for freeze response: Circle, Tether, and all major issuers should commit to 1-hour freeze response for confirmed theft events
3. Supply chain audit for all multi-sig wallet providers: Safe{Wallet} compromise shows third-party UI risk — independent audits required annually
4. "Flood the zone" monitoring rule: unusually high-frequency transactions from new addresses across multiple chains = automatic escalation trigger
5. No-KYC exchange blacklisting: pre-configured block on all known no-KYC instant exchanges (eXch, FixedFloat etc.) pending individual review
6. Protocol fee disgorgement standard: any protocol that earns fees from confirmed illicit flows should have a regulatory obligation to escrow and return
7. Ecosystem-wide bounty coordination: Bybit's $140M bounty worked — standardize bounty programs as AML tool for all major incidents
Key insight: 77% of funds remained traceable — blockchain transparency is an AML asset. The gap is coordination speed, not forensic capability.
ACAMS CAMS 10th Ed. · FATF VA Guidance 2021 · BSA/FinCEN · DORA (EU) · GENIUS Act 2025 · IEEPA

svg.text: Author: Phelipe Agnelli — AML & Blockchain Forensics · Sources: ZachXBT · TRM Labs · Chainalysis · ChainArgos · Nansen · Crystal Intelligence · FBI PSA · NCC Group · ACAMS · FATF
